options.label: B
options.text: The friction force that acts on objects moving through air

options.label: C
options.text: A force that only affects aeroplanes

correct_answer: The friction force that acts on objects moving through air
explanation: Air resistance is a type of friction force that opposes motion when objects move through air. It acts in the opposite direction to the object's movement and is also known as drag. The faster an object moves through air, the greater the air resistance it experiences.
